key_models.res.groups: Security groups. Fields: name, category_id, implied_ids, users, share.
key_models.res.users: User accounts. Fields: login, groups_id, company_id, company_ids, share.
key_models.ir.model.access: Access control lists. Fields: model_id, group_id, perm_read, perm_write, perm_create, perm_unlink.
key_models.ir.rule: Record rules. Fields: model_id, domain_force, groups, global, perm_read, perm_write, perm_create, perm_unlink.
key_models.ir.model: Model metadata. Fields: model, name, access_ids, rule_ids, field_id, state.
key_models.ir.model.fields: Field definitions. Fields: name, model_id, field_description, groups, ttype, relation.
key_models.res.partner: Partner records often subject to privacy and GDPR rules.

crons.privacy_lookup_cron: Privacy data retention and cleanup (if configured)

security_groups.base.group_no_one: Technical Features — access to technical fields
security_groups.base.group_public: Public — anonymous access for public controllers

record_rules.ir_rule_public: Public access rule limiting records for portal/public users

best_practices: Always enforce security on the server side; client-side checks are not enough
Use ir.model.access for model-level permissions and ir.rule for record-level isolation
Avoid global rules when user-specific rules suffice; global rules affect performance
Use groups= attribute on fields sparingly; prefer ACLs and record rules
Never trust user input in controllers; validate and sanitize all parameters
Use Markup and t-out escaping to prevent XSS in QWeb
Protect against CSRF by using type='http' routes with auth='user' or explicit csrf=False
Avoid raw SQL with user input; use parameterized queries or ORM
Use sudo() only when necessary and log the reason
Regularly audit user groups, record rules, and access logs
Implement data retention and anonymization for GDPR compliance
Use field tracking on sensitive fields for audit trail
